name: Sectioning Intent
summary: A document content region — a self-contained, thematic grouping of content (a section/article/aside-style region) within the document body. Distinct from the Layout Intent (app-shell chrome: shell/pane/dock) and from the Hierarchy Intent (tree nesting): sectioning is flat thematic content structure, not application shell or parent/child nesting. The standard fixes the semantic (what a content section is) once, then exposes its presentational treatment as the open-numbered `variant` axis (platform-decisions.md#open-numbered-variants, generalized from Action #1318 to sectioning per #1322/#1323). Structural role differences (a generic section vs a self-contained article vs a tangential aside vs a labelled region) are the native HTML element choice / block polymorphism, never variants.

variant.description: Purely-presentational treatment of a content section's surface, orthogonal to its semantic role and behavior-free (open-numbered-variants, platform-decisions.md#open-numbered-variants — generalized from Action #1318 to sectioning per #1322/#1323/#1338). The listed values are the recommended core set, not a closed enum: an open-numbered axis, so authors mint additional members per the variant contract (a value names a presentational treatment that leaves semantics and behavior unchanged). Optional with a most-permissive default: absent → the conventional flat surface for the section. Surface is a plain attribute consumed by CSS (`[section][variant]`); no wrapper required. Structural differences (an article vs an aside) are the HTML element / block polymorphism, never variants.

description:
What Sectioning Is

A content region — a self-contained, thematic grouping of content within the document body, the structure named by HTML's sectioning content (<section> / <article> / <aside> and a labelled role="region"). It is flat thematic structure: how the readable body of a document is partitioned into meaningful regions.

It is deliberately distinct from two neighbours, and never re-derives either:

  • Layout (Layout Intent) is the application shell — shell / pane / dock chrome regions and their push/overlay/rail behavior. Sectioning is the content inside the shell, not the shell.
  • Hierarchy (Hierarchy Intent) is tree nesting — parent/child treeitems with levels and traversal. Sectioning regions sit side by side; they are not a tree.

Semantic Role Stays Polymorphism

Which kind of section a region is — a generic <section>, a self-contained syndicatable <article>, a tangential <aside>, or a labelled landmark role="region" — is a structural distinction carried by the native HTML element / block polymorphism, not a dimension and not a variant. Picking the right element is a semantic authoring choice the platform already standardizes; this intent does not re-encode it.

Variant

The one configurable axis is presentational: variant (default plain) names how a section's surface is drawn — plain (no chrome), card (an elevated surface), bordered (a ruled outline). It is open-numbered (open-numbered-variants): the listed values are the recommended core set, and authors mint additional members per the variant contract, each naming a presentational treatment that leaves the section's semantics and behavior unchanged. Surface is a plain attribute consumed by CSS ([section][variant]); no wrapper required.
